skill: Systems of two linear equations in two variables
stem:
5x=30y+60

One of the two equations in a system of linear equations is given. The system has no solution. Which equation could be the second equation in this system?
rationale: Choice A is correct. Rewriting 5x = 30y + 60 in slope-intercept form: y = x/6 - 2 (slope 1/6, intercept -2). For no solution, the second line must have the same slope but a different intercept. Choice A: 2y = x/3 + 4 simplifies to y = x/6 + 2, which has the same slope but a different intercept — parallel, no intersection. Choice B simplifies to y = x/6 - 2, the same line (infinite solutions). Choices C and D have different slopes (one intersection).
